title: Refuse unparseable .mcp.json instead of silently clobbering it
context: writeMcpJson was silently replacing corrupt .mcp.json files with fresh configs, destroying other MCP servers the user had registered there. This violates constraint con_8460b6770f and risks data loss. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: writeMcpJson now throws when encountering a non-empty, unparseable .mcp.json file. The caller (init CLI) catches the error, warns the user, and continues. Init no longer silently loses user data.
consequences: Users must manually fix or delete corrupt .mcp.json files to re-register MCP servers
Init continues with a warning instead of failing, preserving UX while protecting data
Error messages guide users on remediation (fix or remove, then re-run)
Behavior now consistent with other config writers in the codebase
alternatives_rejected: Keep silent clobber (violates con_8460b6770f, data loss)
Fail the entire init on corrupt file (worse UX, blocks tool use)
Auto-backup corrupt file (masks problems, adds complexity)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep silent clobber (violates con_8460b6770f, data loss)
Fail the entire init on corrupt file (worse UX, blocks tool use)
Auto-backup corrupt file (masks problems, adds complexity)
